ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: U nìn kpáaǹ u-wanti tin u cááyee kɔkɔ ki cūtìi dūtì Eefiraati mɔɔkpaandi ní ki dū u-ba ki túĺ Galaadi jool watil pú.
translated: The man who went to the river to fetch water saw a woman from Ephraim who had come to draw water, and he went to her and said, “Give me a little water to drink.”

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun Rubeen dāa cūtì kí nyāntí Jooseefi dilɔkɔgindinee, u bàn̄ee u kpá, ní an wùn̄-u tikpil ní u dáaĺ tiwan tin u pēēyee ki cátì.
translated: Reuben went to look for Joseph but could not find him, so he returned and said, “The boy is gone, and I don’t know where to look for him.”

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: Nnyim nìn mɔ̀tīi sìntī ki cá ki tin cáa sāā uŋmasaalaati. Uŋmasaalaati gbanti wimpeepeekaaŋu dalee, ní ajoon gbīitìń nnyimin.
translated: The person who has no child is like a tree that bears no fruit. A childless person is not respected in the community, and their name is quickly forgotten.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ní Jaakɔb bí-u yii: «M gbāam̀-si, tùkù-mi a-yindi.» Ní u bí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a là kí béé m-yindi?» Niin niin ní u bīiǹ Jaakɔb boon.
translated: And Jacob said: “I have seen, therefore I am afraid.” And he said: “What is it that you have seen that makes you afraid?” So Jacob told them everything.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Á cù kuŋɔcaŋkpuŋun kí lēeń iŋɔpɔɔn ili kí cáań-mi ḿ ŋá tijin tin a-baa néeńee
translated: The child who is crying is the one whose mother went to the farm and left him behind.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ náantam nín kíĺ puee dee. Eedeen kpaabun bɔti Ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti nyántì kitiŋ nì kutagbɔŋuee,
translated: The beginning is the most important thing in life. That is why the elders say that the one who starts well will finish well.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: An puee ti-baa gaajati tin Unimbɔti fōō u-ceeyee, timbi nì ti-biyaamu di yì. Min kɔkɔ Unimbɔti yaa bí yii á ŋée, ŋá míǹ.»
translated: The child said to his mother, “Unimbɔti has come to greet you, but he has not brought anything.” Then Unimbɔti himself came and greeted her, saying, “Here I am.”

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: Ama busubu bun yú bukpaa kansikinee bil ní Unimbɔti bí yii tí taa ŋmɔ́ kí taa gbēē-bu, an yaa sá fám ti làá kpú.»
translated: The whole world will know that Unimbɔti is the one who will fight and defeat this war, and he will give us peace.

ref: The seven good cows stand for seven years, and so do the seven good heads of grain.
text: Inasal iluli in ŋān̄ee, taapu di sá abin aluli, ní idifin in ŋān̄ee mun taapu sá abin aluli. Niin sá kudamintibaantiiu ní.
translated: The child is crying, the mother is not giving him food, and the father is also not giving him food. That is why he is crying.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Bi gífì míǹ iwiin ita dal ŋyunti ŋun bi dáá kā iween puee ní Jaakɔb jipɔmbi bili, Simeeyɔn nì Leefii bin sá Diina niijabee yōoǹ bi-tàataajimmu ki ŋá soooo ki kɔ́ kitiŋin ubɔ kaa béè ní ki kūū bininjab kɔkɔ.
translated: When Jacob’s daughter Dinah went out to visit the women of the land, Shechem son of Hamor the Hivite, the ruler of that area, saw her and took her and lay with her by force.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Ní ti-Dindaan bí Jaakɔb yii: «Nín̄ gítí a-naanjab tiŋkin a-dumpu, man làá nīn bí a-cee.»
translated: This is what Jacob said: “This pile of stones is a witness between you and me today.”

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Tiwan tin kɔkɔ pée cáá kufoouee nóoń tɔb tili tili, ki kɔ́ Noowa cee, meela poon.
translated: The child who was born in the morning has grown up quickly, and now Noowa has become a young man.

ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Noowa kpáá meela ki dóòyee ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Á kɔ́ meelan, sii nì a-dicaŋku kɔkɔ, kun puee a-yuntiŋu nib nee kansikinee, sii ní m ká yii a bí deedee.
translated: Now, the elders said this to the people of Dindaan: "If you do not agree, then let the matter be settled by the elders, and whatever they decide will be final, so that it does not come back to you again."

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Ní Saara bí Abraam yii: «Jà uyumbu nee nì u-biki. Di sá yii u-biki nì man biki Isaaki kaa máaǹ ki kpāfì kí jī a-fakal.»
translated: And Sarah said to Abraham: “This is wrong, what you have done. It is wrong for you to send away Hagar and her son along with my son Isaac.”

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Kutaa wúntì ŋwìiń nyáń ní Jaakɔb yíkì Peeniyeel. U-kpìiŋŋu puee, u nìn dúlifí ní.
translated: The place where Jacob wrestled with God is called Peniel. When day broke, he was limping.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Ní u bí u-tɔntɔnliibi bilee yii: «Nimbi ń gbíntí doo nì kimaŋ, man nì m-jipɔɔn ń càatī lapu doo kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti kí fàtīń.»
translated: This is what the spider said: "I will not eat any meat, but I will just sit here and wait for Unimbɔti to come and eat."

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Kí wàatī tí gbìl̄ kí tīī nì nee, asee ní yàntī bin kɔkɔ sá bininjab ni-kansikinee ń gīfì akpancan kii timbi nín gíí puee.
translated: When the time comes for us to leave this world, we will go to the place where our ancestors are, and they will welcome us with joy.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Kutaa wúntèe, Jaakɔb kpēēyee Leeya dee, ní an ŋá-u bitii. Ní u bālifì u-wee Laaban yii: «Ba ŋá ní a ŋá-mi mimmee? An kaa sá Raseel pu ní m kāl̀ a-cee ki túń ditundi ki tīī-saaa? Ba pu ní a kíil̀-mi ki dū Leeya ki tīī-mi?»
translated: Early in the morning, Jacob said to Leah: “You know that I served your father for you. Why have you deceived me and given me Rachel instead? Did I not work for you so that I could marry Leah?”

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: ní bi yìin̄ kitiŋ gbanti poon ki tin bàn̄ laaba bi yíì niin yii Siikeem, ní ki tin nyáń Mooree subu pu. Ŋyunti gbantee, Kanaan yab di nìn dá láá kóò kitiŋ gbantin.
translated: He said that when the sun set and night fell, they would go to Siikeem, and that they would speak Mooree there. The next day, Kanaan went and also set out at sunset.

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Ní m bālifì-u yii: <Kin unimpu yaa kaa là kí nóoń-mi fáà!>
translated: This is my story: <I am going to tell you what happened to me!>

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created the world and everything in it, and he created man. He also created animals and plants.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: If you don't have money to buy that book, and you can't borrow that book, and you don't have any way to read that book, then just leave it and let it rest there.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was a righteous man, and when he was about to sacrifice his son, God sent a ram to replace the boy so that the sacrifice would be complete.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The reason we say that the child has no father is that when Jacob was about to die, he blessed the child who was called the son of his old age, and he did not bless the child who was the son of his youth.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: It is Jacob who came to the farm. He saw that Unimbɔti had harvested the cassava.

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Nnagɔŋgɔmu ŋkpiimu mululi mun páaǹní ipeekaayee boonee, taapu di sá abin aluli mun. Ní ndifiŋkpiimu mululi mun ugbuŋkoon fáá mu fáaḿee taapu sá ŋkùm abin aluli.
translated: The old woman went to the farm to harvest cassava, but she did not find any cassava there. So the young woman went to the river to fetch water, and she also did not find any water there.

ref: The Lord saw how bad the people on earth were and that everything they thought and planned was evil.
text: Ti-Dindaan nìn ká yii binib kpitii mɔ̀tīi wīintí kitiŋ pu. Baabadalee tibɔkpitil ŋma di nìn gbí bi-pɔbiliŋ poon bi dàkafī.
translated: The children went to the farm to harvest corn. Their parents told them not to eat unripe corn.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: M làá bìin̄ u-boon kí yàntī ú màal̀-si kibininjabiki, kí bìin̄ u-boon ú kpántí anibul kupaau na. U-maaliŋu poon ní atimbun tikpil bɔtiibi làá nyā.»
translated: “I have told you that if you do not stop stealing, you will end up in prison. Your mother and I are tired of always paying fines.”

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Ní Amɔɔr bí-bi yii: «Ni-bisal Diina kɔ́ m-jipɔɔn Siikeem tikpil, an puee m gbāam̀-ni, ní dū-u kí tīī-u ú cáá.
translated: Amɔɔr said this: "When Diina went to Siikeem to look for a wife, I went with him, and we stayed there for a long time."

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Niin ní Deeboora un nìn lōō Reebeekee kpíì ní bi síb́-u seeni subu bun yú ki pìlīǹ Beeteelee taapu. Ní Jaakɔb pú busubu gbanti diyindi yii <Nsiim subu.>
translated: This is Deborah and this is Rebecca who died and was buried here, and beside her is Beteli's father. And Jacob also buried his mother here.

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Ní Jooseefi kíí Faaroo yii: «An kaa sá man di ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba, Unimbɔti di làá tùkù-si a-damintiiku taapu ní án sɔ̀ŋkì a-pɔbil.»
translated: And Joseph said to Pharaoh: “The dreams are one; God has revealed to Pharaoh what he is about to do.”

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: Ti yaa gbìl̄ ki tīī nì bi bí nīn kóò ti-ceeyee, bi-waŋkuti kɔkɔ nì bi-gaajati kaa kpántì ti-yati deeee?»
translated: What will we do if our children grow up and ask us, "Where are our fathers and mothers, and why did they leave us like this?"

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Ní ki bí-u yii: «Yīkì kí nīn cá Padan-Aram, a-na baa Beetuweel cee kí kpèè a-wee Laaban sapɔmbi kansikin ubɔ kí cáá.
translated: And he said to them: “I see your father’s face, that he is not favorable toward me as before.”

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Ní Seeti mun màl̄ kibininjabiki ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Eenɔsi. Ŋyunti gbanti ní binib kíĺ ki kíǹ ki yíì Unimbɔti yii ti-Dindaan.
translated: Seeti went to the forest to look for food and found a wild animal. The animal chased her but she escaped and reached the village of Dindaan.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Abraam nìn púĺ tikpil, ní ti-Dindaan mun nìn bīiǹ u-boon ajan kɔkɔ pu.
translated: Abraham was very old, and the Lord had blessed him in everything.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: Unimbɔti came and took the child from the woman, and the woman also took the child from Unimbɔti. They both took the child together.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: He said that the book was not good, that it was very bad, and that he would not read that book again, because it had hurt his feelings.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was sitting under a tree, and he saw three men standing before him, and he ran to meet them and bowed down to the ground.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The reason we say that our ancestors came and met this land is that Jacob came and settled here with his people, and we say that our ancestors met him living here with his people.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: It is Jacob who came to the farm. He saw that Unimbɔti had already harvested the corn.

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: «Aayee, ukpil! Pīlifiǹ-mi, maa làá nyāfiǹ-si kusaau nì ntaŋkpalɔkɔ ŋun bí ku-poonee. M làá dūú pɔ̄-si ní m-nabiyaamu kɔkɔ nimbiin, á cù kí síb́ a-nimpuu.»
translated: "Aayee, ukpil! Pīlifiǹ-mi, I will not let you go today because of the debt you owe me. I will seize something of yours until you pay me back, and then I will release it to you."

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: U nìn yākatì tiwaŋkutee akiti akiti ki dūu tīī u-tɔntɔnliibi ní ki bí-bi yii: «Lìntīmaan kí nīn cá kí nīn pá tɔb boon ki dátiǹ tɔb.»
translated: The child went to the river to fetch water and saw a frog that said to him: "My friend, give me some water to drink."

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Ní unaaciinee bālifì-u yii: «Bi yíì-si yii ba ní?» Ní u kíí-u yii: «Bi yíì-mi yii Jaakɔb ní.»
translated: The man asked him: “What is your name?” And he said: “My name is Jacob.”

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Raseel nín màl̄ Jooseefi boonee ní Jaakɔb bí Laaban yii: «Pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ fàtīí nīn kùǹ m-tiŋkin.
translated: Send me word to Joseph that Jacob said to Laban: "Let me go back to my place and to my land."

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Ní Abraam sútì ki pìl̄ ti-Dindaan ní ki bí-u yii: «Mimmee, a làá kúntí binikpitib kí kpáaǹ deedeedambaaa?
translated: Abraham spoke to the Lord and said: "Behold, I have no child; what will you give me?"

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Ŋyunti ŋun Jooseefi bāań bi-ceeyee ní bi pēetì-u u-bɔkutimɔntiil din u pēēyee.
translated: Joseph's wife went to the farm and found a big yam.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Di sá yii bi nìn cáá tigaajati tikpil, ní kitiŋ kin poon bi kóòyee kaa nìn gíti bà-bi, kun puee bi-waŋkuti nìn būtì tikpil.
translated: When the sun rises, we go to the farm, and when it sets, we return home, then we rest after the day's work.

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: Ní m ŋúb́ Faaroo sambinyɔkaaki m-ŋaan, ki kītì fiinyi biliŋee ki ŋmáaḿ ŋi-nyim ki ŋá Faaroo sambinyɔkaakin ní ki dūu tīī-u.»
translated: It is said that when Faroo was about to die, he called his children and said to them, “I, Faroo, am about to die, and this house is for you.”

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: M cáá inaa nì mmamu nì ipii nì iŋoobu nì bitɔntɔnliibi bininjab nì binimpoobi. An pu ní m túnní bí dāá tùkù sii m-kpiliu yii á dàkà-mi a-ŋanti kí fōō-mi.>»
translated: We have come to the world, we have come to stay, we have come to grow old, and we will return to the earth. No one knows the day death will come and take him away, so let us always be prepared.

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Ní ki gíti bí yii: «Ŋma di nìn ŋūǹ kí lì Abraam cee yii man Saara dāa bí kí ŋmàb̄ kibiki? Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m màl̄-u kibininjabiki u-puti poon!» Bi jìn̄ Agaar nì u-biki Isimaayeel
translated: And she said: “Who would have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age!” And Hagar bore him Ishmael.

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: m làá dāá téetí dipoondi din dɔ̄ man nì ni nì tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoouee kansikinee pu. Di sá yii nnyiŋgbeen kaa ti bí kí dāá kúntí tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoouee.
translated: The person who was bitten by the dog yesterday is the one who came to the hospital today. It is because of this that we say the dog that bit him yesterday is the one that came to the hospital.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: U yaa lī yii: <Tiwaŋkuti tin kɔkɔ cáá atanee di sá a-paatee>, tiwaŋkuti kɔkɔ màal̀ tin cáá atanee ní. U yaa tí lī yii: <Tiwaŋkuti tin kɔkɔ cáá iluntoonee di sá a-paatee>, tiwaŋkuti kɔkɔ tí màal̀ tin cáá iluntoonee ní.
translated: He said this: “If we had known that the matter would end up like this, we would have handled the matter differently.” He also said this: “If we had known that the affair would turn out like this, we would have managed the affair differently.”

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Ní gbíntí kí nīn kā ti-cee, ní ti-tiŋki ń kpántí ni-yaka ní nīn kóò, kí nīn tɔ́ ni-ba kí bàntì kí kpántí bitindambi.»
translated: If you want to go to the market, you must take the path that leads there, and you must not forget to bring your basket.

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Ní u-mambi mɔ̄tìi díkí u-pu, ama u-baa úǹ dū an bɔtee ki ŋá u-taalin. Jooseefi mambi dū-u ki nyáfì
translated: Joseph's mother is not his father's wife, but she is the one who gave birth to him and raised him. Joseph's mother is his aunt.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Dalbee ní u nyùn̄ fiinyi subiliŋ daam ki gbí ní ki dɔ̄ u-tantiu poon dicancaŋgbeel.
translated: Dalbee is a young man who went to the farm and found a strange animal.

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni yaa lafun kíí tiŋman yii ḿ síb́ m-nimpuuee, ní gbàam̀ Soowaar jipɔɔn Eefrɔn,
translated: He said to him: “I am a foreigner living among you; give me a burial site among you, so that I may bury my dead,” namely Sarah, from before Ephron.

ref: Finally, they reached Bethel, also known as Luz.
text: Ní Jaakɔb nì u-nipaaku dūu bàn̄ Luusi, an dàkà yii Beeteel, Kanaan tiŋkin.
translated: And Jacob said to Luusi, “I am your relative from the land of Canaan, Beeteel.”

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: M là á pùtī ti-Dindaan yilpu nì taapu Nimbɔtiu yii aa làá cáá Kanaan yab bin kansikin m kóò nee sapɔmbi ubɔ kí tīī m-jipɔɔn.
translated: The people of Dindaan say that when Nimbɔtiu came to the land of Canaan, he found no one there except the one who had created the world.

ref: Esau took his children and wives, his relatives and servants, his animals and possessions he had acquired while in Canaan, and moved far from Jacob.
text: Boonee Eesayuu yōoǹ u-nimpoobi nì u-jipɔmbi nì u-bisalib nì binib bin kɔkɔ pée bí u-dicaŋkunee, nì u-waŋkuti kɔkɔ nì tigaajati tin kɔkɔ u kāl̀ Kanaan tiŋkin ki kée ki yíkìi búntì kitiŋ kiba poon ki wāatì u-naal Jaakɔb.
translated: Then Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, along with his livestock and all his animals and all the goods he had acquired in Canaan, and he went to a land away from his brother Jacob.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Ní u yāntì bi cúú Jooseefi ki cáa tɔ́ sarka laakin ubɔti nìn pìintí bin yīntiǹ-uee. Jooseefi bí sarka Jooseefi kpántì usarkadaan.
translated: It was not because Joseph's brothers loved him, but they hated him because of his dreams. Joseph told his dreams to his brothers.

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Ní u bí unimpu yii: «A nín ŋá miŋyee, m làá gbéeǹ-si timatíǹdi gìńdi. Nì falaa ní a làá nīn màal̀ a-biyaamu. A-cal neeliŋu làá nīn pée cáá-si, ama úǹ di làá nīn cáá sii pu mpɔn.»
translated: This is what the child said: “Mother, I am going; I will not stay in this village. I am going to look for my father’s family. I will go and stay with my mother’s brother, but you must not stay here alone.”

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Dalbee, Abraam yíiń u-tɔntɔnliu un yúntì u-cee ki sá ukpil ki kpèé u-wanti kɔkɔee ní ki bí-u yii: «Dū a-ŋal kí tíkiń m-tapandi taapu.
translated: Dalbee, Abraham's father-in-law, took his daughter and gave her to his son as a wife, saying to him: "Go, I have given you my daughter as wife."

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Bi nìn yíì dibuntaatataatiilee yii Tiiga. Díǹ di pùubìń Asuur tiŋkin, ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú. Dibuntaananaatiil ní bi yíì Eefiraati.
translated: This is the first chapter of the book of Genesis. In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth. The earth was without form and void.

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: «Aayee, ukpil! Pīlifiǹ-mi, maa làá nyāfiǹ-si kusaau nì ntaŋkpalɔkɔ ŋun bí ku-poonee. M làá dūú pɔ̄-si ní m-nabiyaamu kɔkɔ nimbiin, á cù kí síb́ a-nimpuu.»
translated: "Aayee, ukpil! Pīlifiǹ-mi, I have come to tell you that the blacksmith who was here has left. I have come to take the hoe that I left with you, and to take my things."

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: See á tɔ̄ ditundi kí káaĺ kutuntuŋu a-yigbeel pu ní kí wàatī kí nín ká tijin kí jī. Án làá nīn pée bí-si míǹ ní, áli á dāá fàtīí gítí ntam min m lēē ki máń-see poon. Kun puee a sá ŋkpɔŋkpɔm ní ki làá fàtīí gítí ŋkpɔŋkpɔm.»
translated: The child went to the farm and found a bird's nest, took it and came home and gave it to his mother. His mother said to him, "If you had left it there, its mother would have brought many more birds." So it is with knowledge: if you leave it where it is, it will multiply.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Meelkiiseedeeki nìn mēē ki bīiǹ u-pu ki lī yii: «Yilpu fɔ́ŋŋŋ Nimbɔtiu un náań yilpu nì taapuee ń bìin̄ Abram boon.
translated: Melchizedek brought out bread and wine; he was a priest of God Most High, and he blessed Abram, saying, “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.”

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Ní Juuda bí u-coopu Taamaar yii: «Cù a-baabi cee kí nīn kā dikpiipool ki cí ŋyunti ŋun m-jipɔɔn Seera mun làá dāá wíiǹee.» Di sá u nìn lī u-neen yii: «Seera mun ń taa kpú kii u-mambee.» Ní Taamaar nín kùǹ u-baabi.
translated: And Judah said to his friend Tamar: "Your father-in-law has told me to give you this kid from the flock that is with me, saying: 'Have sex with your brother's wife.'" But she said to him: "My brother is not willing to take me as his wife." And Tamar went to her father-in-law.

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Ní Abimeeleeki bí-u yii: «Maa nyí un di túń an tundee. Si mun a-ba kaa lī tibɔti gbanti m-cee, maa tí gbìl̄-tì ubɔ cee dalba, see díǹ.»
translated: Abimelech said to him: “You are a stranger among us. If any man should harm you, we will give you seven men from our clan, and they will die in your place.”

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ní unaaciinee bí-u yii: «Baa làá ti yíiń-si Jaakɔb, bi làá nīn kíǹ ki yíì-si yii Isirayeel ní. Di sá yii si nì Unimbɔti mɔ̀n̄ ní si nì binib mun mɔ̀n̄, ní a pɔ́ɔkiǹ bi-kɔkɔ.»
translated: The Lord said this: “You will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have struggled with God and with men, and you have prevailed.”

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Ní u-baa kíí-u yii: «M-biki, Unimbɔti u-ba di làá tin tīī-ti dipibil din ti làá dūú ŋá saraawɔkaayee.» Ní bi nín nìkì bi-saŋŋu.
translated: He said to him: "My child, Unimbɔti has said that we should take these books and go to the school together." And they went and did it.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: U dāa yáatì ki kpēē nyɔkɔpuee, bininjab bita di yú kaa dátiǹ-uee. U ká-bee ní ki yíkì u-tantiu nyɔkɔpu ki sáǹ ki túŋkì-bi. U bàn̄ bi-ceeyee, ní ki gbāaǹ ki jáam̀-bi.
translated: He went to the farm to harvest yams, but he did not find any ripe ones. He returned home and told his wife that the yams were not yet ready. He sat on the veranda, and his wife prepared lunch.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: U jáam̀ ti-Dindaan ki dóòyee ní ki nyántìń animbil kúti wanti, nì sindaariya wanti nì tiwampeeŋkaal ki tīī Reebeeka, ní ki tīī u-niija nì u-na mun tigaajawan tikpil.
translated: Rebecca and her maids got on their camels and followed the servant, while the man bowed down and worshiped the Lord.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Tinaakɔti tin kɔkɔ nyánní Noowa maaliŋunee dee, kunaakoou kamaa nì ku-maaliŋu nì ku-timbuŋŋi. Ki dī bíǹ pu ní atimbun kɔkɔ yàl̄ ki gbí kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu nnyiŋgbeen boon.
translated: We thank Noowa for the good things he has done, because he is the one who does good and saves. He has given us salvation and has also given us eternal life.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: It is Jacob who is my younger brother. He said that God has given him a child from an old woman.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Dikuntinyindee yaa nyáń ki làl̄ atagban pu m wál̀-dèe, ní ḿ téetí dipoondi din dɔ̄ kí nīn cá jaanjaan, man Unimbɔti nì tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoou ki bí kitiŋ puee kansikinee pu.»
translated: The elders say that if a child does not listen to advice, when he grows up and becomes a young man, he will regret it and remember the words of Unimbɔti, who told him that laziness does not lead to a good future.
